title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Accessibility Charter
summary: A team says “build a maze solver” without defining the player task, puzzle rules, assistance, supported input, repository state, or proof of success. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility charter increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 9260 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs. A defensible solution applies Accessibility Charter, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-accessibility-charter — Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_outcomes_repo_accessibility_2(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-accessibility-charter-1bajku
# Competency: maze-accessibility-charter.
# Case 4577: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_accessibility_charter_1bajku(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bajku = "1bajku-ukjab1-4577"
    assert evidence_variant_1bajku
    evidence_axes_1bajku = (
        "grid1bajku",
        "topology4577",
        "searchukjab1",
        "event1bajku4577",
        "access45771bajku",
        "recoveryukjab14577",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bajku)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessibility Charter: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 6354 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha….
steps.why: Retrieving Accessibility Charter through the concrete player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, keep Accessibility Charter connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Accessibility Charter precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 6354 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Accessibility can be added after the maze looks attractive.
